name: Spellcasting (Wizard)
scalingSummary: Full caster; prepared from spellbook (Intelligence); 6 spells at L1, +2 per level
mechanicalSummary: Prepare spells from a personal spellbook using Intelligence. Learn 6 spells at L1, +2 per level. Can ritually cast unprepared ritual spells from spellbook. Full caster slot progression.

evidence.pointer: Wizard casterType=full, prepared caster with spellbook learning

name: Arcane Recovery
rechargeSummary: Once per long rest (during short rest)
scalingSummary: Recover combined spell levels = half wizard level (round up), max 5th-level slots
mechanicalSummary: During a short rest, recover expended spell slots totaling up to half your wizard level (rounded up). No slot can be 6th level or higher.

name: Spell Mastery
mechanicalSummary: Choose a 1st-level and a 2nd-level wizard spell. Can cast them at their lowest level without expending spell slots. Can change choices after 8 hours of study.

name: Signature Spells
mechanicalSummary: Choose two 3rd-level wizard spells as signature spells. Always prepared, and can cast each once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot per short or long rest.

name: Arcane Ward (School of Abjuration)
scalingSummary: Ward HP = 2×wizard level + Int modifier; regenerates 2×spell level when casting abjuration spells
mechanicalSummary: Gain a magical ward absorbing damage. When casting an abjuration spell, ward regains HP equal to twice spell level. At L6, can use reaction to have ward absorb damage for an ally within 30 ft.

evidence.pointer: school-of-abjuration L2: Arcane Ward; L6: Projected Ward

notes: Projected Ward at L6 is a reaction.

name: Projected Ward (School of Abjuration)
trigger: When a creature within 30 ft takes damage
mechanicalSummary: Use reaction to have your Arcane Ward absorb the damage instead of the targeted ally.

name: Portent (School of Divination)
trigger: When any creature you can see makes an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check
scalingSummary: 2 pre-rolled d20s at L2, 3 at L14
mechanicalSummary: After each long rest, pre-roll 2 d20s (3 at L14). Before any attack/save/check result is declared, you can replace the d20 roll with one of your portent dice.
notes: No action cost — reactive replacement. One of the strongest wizard features.

name: Hypnotic Gaze (School of Enchantment)
rechargeSummary: Once per creature per long rest
mechanicalSummary: As an action, choose a creature within 5 ft. Wis save or charmed and incapacitated with speed 0. Maintain as action each subsequent turn. Ends if target takes damage or can't see/hear you.

name: Instinctive Charm (School of Enchantment)
trigger: When a creature within 30 ft makes an attack roll against you
rechargeSummary: Once per creature per long rest
mechanicalSummary: Use reaction to force the attacker (if not immune to charmed) to make Wis save. On failure, redirect the attack to the closest creature (other than you or itself).

name: Sculpt Spells (School of Evocation)
scalingSummary: Protect up to 1 + spell level creatures
mechanicalSummary: When casting an evocation spell, choose 1 + spell level creatures to automatically succeed their save and take no damage if they would normally take half.
notes: Passive modification of evocation spell casting.

name: Overchannel (School of Evocation)
trigger: When casting a wizard evocation spell of 1st-5th level that deals damage
resourceCostSummary: No resource cost; self-damage on reuse
rechargeSummary: Once per long rest free; subsequent uses cause d12 necrotic damage per spell level
mechanicalSummary: Maximize the damage of a 1st-5th level evocation spell. Second use before a long rest deals necrotic damage per spell level (cumulative).

name: Illusory Self (School of Illusion)
trigger: When a creature makes an attack roll against you
mechanicalSummary: Use reaction to create an illusory duplicate. The attack automatically misses.

name: Grim Harvest (School of Necromancy)
trigger: When killing a creature with a spell of 1st level or higher
scalingSummary: HP regain = 2× spell level (3× if necromancy spell)
mechanicalSummary: Regain HP equal to twice the spell's level (or 3× if necromancy) when killing a creature with a spell. Doesn't work on constructs or undead.

name: Transmuter's Stone (School of Transmutation)
rechargeSummary: Can be remade on long rest; destroyed if new one made
mechanicalSummary: Spend 8 hours creating a transmuter's stone granting one benefit to holder: darkvision 60 ft, +10 speed, proficiency in Con saves, or resistance to one of acid/cold/fire/lightning/thunder. Can change the benefit when casting a transmutation spell of 1st+.

name: Bladesong (Bladesinging)
mechanicalSummary: As a bonus action, enter bladesong for 1 minute. Gain: +Int to AC, +10 ft walking speed, advantage on Acrobatics, +Int to Con saves for concentration. Ends on incapacitation, donning medium/heavy armor/shield, or two-handed weapon use.

name: Song of Defense (Bladesinging)
resourceCostSummary: Expend a spell slot; reduce damage by 5× spell level
rechargeSummary: Spell slots recharge on long rest
mechanicalSummary: When taking damage while Bladesong is active, expend a spell slot to reduce the damage by 5× the slot's level.

name: Arcane Deflection (War Magic)
trigger: When hit by an attack or failing a saving throw
mechanicalSummary: Use reaction to gain +2 AC against that attack or +4 on that saving throw. Cannot cast spells other than cantrips until end of next turn.

name: Power Surge (War Magic)
trigger: When dealing damage with a wizard spell
resourceCostSummary: 1 use (free; gain one when you dispel or counterspell, or on short rest)
rechargeSummary: Regain 1 on short rest; gain one when using Dispel Magic or Counterspell
scalingSummary: Extra force damage = half wizard level
mechanicalSummary: Deal extra force damage equal to half wizard level to one target of a spell. Max one surge per spell.

name: Chronal Shift (School of Chronurgy)
trigger: When a creature within 30 ft makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw (after seeing the roll)
mechanicalSummary: Use reaction to force the creature to reroll the d20. Must use the new roll.

name: Momentary Stasis (School of Chronurgy)
mechanicalSummary: As an action, force a Large or smaller creature within 60 ft to make Con save or be incapacitated with speed 0 until end of your next turn. On success, creature has half speed and can take one of action/bonus/reaction.
